source_jobs.title: Nurse Practitioner, Special Interest in Weight Management (20 hours per week)
source_jobs.description:
About Felix

Founded in 2019, Felix is a digital healthcare company that puts the patient in control of their own health and wellbeing. We’re passionate about building technology and beautiful customer experiences to make healthcare more accessible (and more enjoyable) for all Canadians.
Felix is Canada's first end-to-end healthcare experience, providing online treatment, prescription delivery and ongoing care. We deliver care across a growing range of daily health categories such as weight loss, birth control, menopause, erectile dysfunction, acne and more.‍



Description

We are seeking Nurse Practitioners with strong generalist experience and a passion for weight management to join our growing team of healthcare providers. This role requires a minimum commitment of 20 hours per week. If that sounds like you, we want to hear from you!



You must be licensed as a Nurse Practitioner in at least one province in Canada. Preference will be given to NPs with multiple licensing, or with licensing that meets our current needs.



Advantages of joining Felix:

 - 100% remote: from the comfort of your home, on your laptop, computer or even on your mobile and tablets.

 - Leading virtual medicine provider in Canada: work at the cutting edge of virtual healthcare

 - Professional Development: plenty of opportunities to connect and learn from some of Canada’s leading physicians in weight loss, menopause and virtual care.

 - Safety is our priority: our platform is secure for both the patient and health professional and has been purpose built to comply with applicable health information privacy regulations.

 - Intuitive platform: the Felix platform is easy to use, plus HCPs have continuous on-hand engineering and clinical support available to them.



Who you are:

 - Felix is seeking a licensed Nurse Practitioner in at least one province in Canada, preferably with cross-jurisdictional licensure.

 - You are available to provide a minimum of 20 hours of scheduled time per week on the platform.

 - You have 2+ years of Nurse Practitioner experience, with demonstrated experience of providing patients with the best possible care.

 - You have direct experience with treating and managing obesity.

 - You are physically located in Canada, with a billing number in the province(s) that you are licensed, required for ordering laboratory testing.



 - You are a member in good standing of the College(s) where you hold the title of Nurse Practitioner, maintaining eligibility and willingness to be licensed in other provinces.

 - You maintain confidentiality and follow the code of conduct as per the governing body of their province/territory.

 - You have experience in telemedicine. Additional experience in weight loss, menopause, mental health, erectile dysfunction, and birth control care are assets.

 - You have a proficient understanding of technology, EMRs and remote work.

 - You are adaptable and open to change.



Compensation

 - Base weekly rate, with additional assessment remuneration tied to services delivered above a defined threshold.

source_jobs.title: Production Coordinator
source_jobs.description:
About Felix

Felix is Canada’s first end-to-end platform providing on-demand treatment for everyday health. Felix creates digital-first solutions that increase access for common healthcare needs such as sexual health, weight loss, testing and diagnostics, and more. Founded in 2019, Felix’s approach to healthcare encompasses assessments, prescriptions, fast and discreet delivery, and ongoing healthcare support — all online.



Felix has delivered 24 quarters of consecutive growth in both revenue and profit, making it one of Canada’s fastest growing tech companies. But more importantly, more than 1 million Canadians trust Felix for their everyday healthcare needs.



Creative at Felix

At Felix, we believe that creative expression is at the core of achieving our mission. By demystifying complexity, removing stigma, and increasing access, we can deliver care that truly puts patients first.

You’ll be joining a tight-knit team of marketers and creatives that collaborate closely to create a beautiful and cohesive experience for patients throughout their journey.



The Role

We are seeking a Production Coordinator with a strong understanding of performance marketing, creator and production talent sourcing and management, and production workflows to join our team. Reporting to the associate creative director, you will collaborate closely with our team of strategists, designers, video editors, and content creators to bring concepts to life. This role requires exceptional organization, time management, and communication skills, as you will manage a high volume of creative under tight timelines while maintaining a high standard of quality.



In this role, you will:

 - Build and maintain a vetted roster of creators, actors, photographers, videographers, and production assistants, complete with rate cards, availability, and specialties

 - Orchestrate weekly project plans and daily schedules, ensuring creative deliverables hit every deadline from ideation to launch

 - Negotiate rates, issue SOWs, and track POs/invoices to stay within defined budgets

 - Source locations, props, and wardrobe as needed; oversee production days when required

 - Translate creative briefs into clear shot lists, call sheets, timelines, and deliverable checklists

 - Brief content creators, actors, and creative partners, including photographers, videographers, editors, and designers, while maintaining strong working relationships

 - Run post-shoot feedback loops to identify improvement opportunities and ensure consistency across deliverables

 - Secure all required usage rights, model releases, and compliance documentation before production begins

 - Ensure deliverables meet the brief, adhere to brand guidelines, ad platform specs, and compliance requirements as needed

 - Perform first-level QC, identify pacing, story flow, or visual issues and coordinate quick turnarounds with editors and creators




We’re looking for someone who:

 - Has 3–5 years in creative project management, production, or operations

 - Has a proven track record of improving creative processes in a growth-stage environment

 - Experience in the Performance Marketing industry is a huge plus

 - Is extremely organized and can manage a variety of creative requests and deliverables, from a wide range of creative partners and stakeholders

 - Has strong project management skills and is an exceptional communicator

 - Maintains strict adherence to industry regulations and legal requirements

 - Is a self-starter and can juggle multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment

 - Obsesses over details and has a genuine passion for continuously optimizing the production processes

 - Is kind and empathetic, and genuinely excited about the possibilities in the healthcare space




Benefits

 - Full medical, dental and vision benefits

 - Maternity/paternity policy

 - 4 weeks vacation (3 weeks anytime + 1 week during the holidays)

 - Competitive compensation

 - Stock option grant

source_jobs.title: Nurse Practitioner - Prince Edward Island
source_jobs.description:
About Felix

Founded in 2019, Felix is a digital healthcare company that puts the patient in control of their own health and wellbeing. We’re passionate about building technology and beautiful customer experiences to make healthcare more accessible (and more enjoyable) for all Canadians.

Felix is Canada's first end-to-end healthcare experience, providing online treatment, prescription delivery and ongoing care. We deliver care across a growing range of daily health categories such as weight loss, birth control, menopause, erectile dysfunction, acne and more.‍

 

Description

We are looking for innovative Nurse Practitioners to join our growing team of healthcare providers. At the cutting edge of virtual care, Felix connects patients with medical professionals to help them address and treat their lifestyle health concerns.

If you are interested in joining a team of healthcare practitioners and are driven to provide quality online care across Canada, then check out the advantages to joining our team!

We are currently looking for a Nurse Practitioner licensed in Prince Edward Island. Preference will be given to NPs with additional licensing.

Advantages of joining Felix:

 - Work from the comfort of your home, on your laptop, computer. You can even work on the go via our HCP portal available on mobiles or iPads.

 - Flexible hours: you pick the hours you want to work (weekends, evenings).

 - Take the vacation you want, when you want it.

 - Join a community of healthcare professionals, working at the cutting edge of virtual healthcare, to share and learn from each other.

 - Opportunities to connect with some of Canada’s leading physicians in Weight Loss, Menopause and virtual care.

 - Easy to use platform, with on hand engineering and clinical support.

 - A platform that is secure for both the patient and health professional, which has been purpose built to comply with PHIPA privacy regulations.

 


Who you are

 - Felix is seeking a Nurse Practitioner with license in Prince Edward Island (PEI), preferably with cross-jurisdictional licensure.

 - You have 2+ years of Nurse Practitioner experience, with demonstrated experience of providing patients with the best possible care.

 - You are physically located in Prince Edward Island (PEI), with a PEI billing number, required for ordering laboratory testing.

 - You are available to provide a minimum of 4 hours of scheduled time per week on the platform, in addition to logging in every 24-48hrs to address patient needs.

 - You are a member in good standing of the College(s) where they hold the title of Nurse Practitioner, maintaining eligibility and willingness to be licensed in other provinces.

 - You maintain confidentiality and follow the code of conduct as per the governing body of their province/territory.

 - You have experience in telemedicine. Additional experience in weight loss, menopause, mental health, ED, and birth control care are assets.

 - You have a proficient understanding of technology, EMRs and remote work.

 - You are adaptable and open to change.


Compensation

 - Nurse practitioners are compensated on a per assessment basis


We are proud to be an equal opportunity workplace committed to building a team culture that celebrates diversity and inclusion.

source_jobs.title: Product Engineer, AI - Native
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT FELIX

Founded in 2019, Felix is a digital healthcare company that puts the patient in control of their own health and well-being. We’re passionate about building technology and beautiful customer experiences to make healthcare more accessible (and more enjoyable) for all Canadians.

Felix is Canada's first end-to-end healthcare experience, providing online treatment, prescription delivery and ongoing care. We deliver care across a growing range of daily health categories such as weight loss, birth control, menopause, erectile dysfunction, acne and more.



DESCRIPTION

Felix is looking for a Product Engineer who can build excellent software, think deeply about product, and care about UX.

This is not a “take tickets and write code” role. You will own problems end to end: understand the user, shape the outcome, make the technical calls, and ship work you are proud of.

AI should already be a default part of how you work. We want someone who uses AI to move faster, prototype sooner, raise the quality bar, and make better product and engineering decisions.





WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Own problems from ambiguity through launch.

 - Turn vague problems into clear product and technical decisions.

 - Collaborate closely with product, design, engineering, clinical, pharmacy, and operations teams.

 - Build across frontend, backend, data, AI integrations, and infrastructure as needed.

 - Work with Vue, PHP/Laravel, Python and supporting systems.

 - Build AI-powered product experiences that are useful, reliable, fast, and obvious to use.

 - Sweat the details: UX, copy, latency, edge cases, empty states, accessibility, privacy, and failure modes.

 - Improve reliability through testing, observability, monitoring, and thoughtful system design.

 - Mentor engineers and help raise the quality of product and technical execution.

 - Own what you ship.
   


REQUIREMENTS

 - Ideally 5+ years of practical full-stack engineering experience operating at a Senior or equivalent technical level.

 - Strong full-stack experience across frontend, backend, and data-oriented systems.

 - Track record of shipping meaningful product features end to end.

 - Strong product judgment and comfort making decisions under ambiguity.

 - Strong communication around technical tradeoffs, scope, product decisions, and risk.

 - Good judgment around reliability, testing, observability, performance, accessibility, privacy, and maintainability.

 - Comfort using AI tools as part of your day-to-day engineering workflow.
   


YOU MIGHT BE A GOOD FIT IF YOU:

 - Have built AI-powered user experiences, AI-assisted workflows, internal AI tools, or AI-enabled operational systems.

 - Can show a portfolio, repo, demo, write-up, or shipped product that demonstrates how you think.

 - Have strong opinions about what makes software feel great, and you can defend them.

 - Have taken a vague problem and turned it into a clear, shipped outcome.

 - Notice product friction, unclear copy, slow flows, broken edge cases, and unnecessary complexity without being asked.
   


WHAT THIS ROLE IS NOT

 - This is not prompt engineering.

 - This is not a narrow frontend or backend role.

 - This is not for someone who waits for perfect specs.

 - This is not for someone who ships whatever an AI tool gives them.
   


BENEFITS

 - Full medical, dental, and vision benefits.

 - Maternity and paternity policy.

 - 4 weeks vacation.

 - Competitive compensation.

 - Stock option grant.

 - Remote-first work from anywhere in Canada.

 - High-impact work at a growing digital healthcare company.

 - Collaborative, high-performing team culture.
   


SENIORITY

This role is designed for engineers who can operate with a high degree of ownership, judgment, and independence. In practice, that usually means senior-level experience, but we care more about demonstrated impact than title or years of experience.

We are also open to exceptional intermediate candidates who have strong agency, produ
